economic_notes: IIFN39 Holdings LP formed August 2019 carrying on various economic development and commercial enterprises. Shoal Lake Landing (retail/landing operation) and Mr. Bubbles Laundromat operated by the First Nation. Subsequent to year-end, CMHC committed mortgage funds up to $805,000 for construction of three new housing units. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event with unknown impact.
notes: Audit is qualified due to incomplete tangible capital asset assessment — only assets acquired since 1996 are recorded, useful lives not reviewed, TCA amounts may be incomplete. IIFN39 Holdings LP (100% owned GBE) accounted for on modified equity basis with net loss of $64,423 for partial period Aug-Dec 2019. Long-term debt includes capital lease obligations of $1,646,221 and long-term debt of $2,293,157 totalling $3,939,378. Annual debt service of $76,217 reflects only long-term debt principal repayments; capital lease repayments of $150,816 not included in that figure. Net financial assets is net debt of $(2,192,482) per Statement of Changes in Net Debt. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements provided. Prior year TCA net book value corrected to $13,333,518 per Note 11.
